The commatrix test itself passed (finished.json: `"result":"SUCCESS"`).

### Test 1: `[TestE2e] Validation [It] should validate the communication matrix ports match the node's open ports`

- **Duration:** 44.471 seconds (within the overall 527.77s suite run)

  Triggered at `validation_test.go:161` via `Fail(fmt.Sprintf("the following ports are used but don't have an endpointslice: \n %s", missingEPSMat))`

- **Root Cause:** The `crio` container runtime (PID 2708) is listening on ephemeral TCP port 46483 bound to the node's external IP `10.0.95.144:46483`. This port falls in the Linux ephemeral range (32768–60999) and has no corresponding Kubernetes EndpointSlice. The test at `validation_test.go:157–162` calls `filterOutPortsOfKnownServices` (which only skips `rpc.statd`, `dnsmasq`, and `dig`), then `filterOutPortsInDynamicRanges` at line 159 — but `commatrix.DynamicRanges` only covers the Kubelet node port range 30000–32767 (the sole remaining entry after PR #400). Port 46483 is not in that range and is not in the known-services filter, so it passes through and triggers the `Fail`. PR #400 ("Remove Linux Dynamic ranges from generated communication matrix", merged 2026-04-28) removed the Linux ephemeral port range (32768–60999) from `ComMatrix.DynamicRanges` because it was causing all ephemeral ports to be open in the nftables firewall rules. A side effect is that the test's `filterOutPortsInDynamicRanges` call no longer screens out crio's ephemeral listening port.

- **Breaking PR/Commit:** https://github.com/openshift-kni/commatrix/pull/400 — "Remove Linux Dynamic ranges from generated communication matrix" (merged 2026-04-28, commit `7d56343`). This PR removed the `DynamicRangeList` covering 32768–60999, which had been implicitly used by the validation test to suppress ephemeral port noise.

- **Source File:** https://github.com/openshift-kni/commatrix/blob/main/test/e2e/validation_test.go#L141 — test body; the failing assertion is at [L161](https://github.com/openshift-kni/commatrix/blob/main/test/e2e/validation_test.go#L161). The filtering functions are at L221–259.

The specific port number changes each run, but the validation will always fail after PR #400 because no filtering rule covers the Linux ephemeral range anymore. This port is captured by `ss` and appears in `ss-generated-matrix.csv`, but because `crio` does not expose a Kubernetes EndpointSlice, the port is absent from the EPS-based `communication-matrix.csv`. The diff file `matrix-diff-ss` therefore marks it with a `-` prefix ("open on node but not in EPS matrix"), placing it in `missingEPSMat`. The test's `filterOutPortsOfKnownServices` function at `test/e2e/validation_test.go:221` only skips `rpc.statd`, `dnsmasq`, and `dig` — it does not skip `crio`. With no filter catching it, the `Fail()` at line 161 fires: `Fail(fmt.Sprintf("the following ports are used but don't have an endpointslice: \n %s", missingEPSMat))`.
